title: Extract msg.sig as a uint256 via shr(224, calldataload(0)) for cheap selector dispatch
description: calldataload(0) reads the first 32 bytes of calldata. The 4-byte function selector occupies the top 4 bytes (offsets 0-3). shr(224, ...) shifts those 4 bytes down into a clean uint256 in the range [0, 2^32). This produces a numerical comparison-friendly selector for or-chained eq() dispatch in fallback functions, avoiding the function-table jump overhead the compiler generates for public functions.
when_to_apply: Fallback functions handling many selectors (token receiver mixins, account abstraction routers) where function-table dispatch overhead matters
when_not_to_apply: Regular public/external functions — the compiler's function table is highly optimized for typical contracts
